name: Magnolia Pickleball
description: Magnolia Pickleball is a place to play pickleball in Madison, MS. There are 7 hard courts. 3 are indoors and 4 are outdoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to locker rooms, restrooms, water, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, wheelchair accessibility, and food. The courts are private. Reservations are not available.
fees: Private — $75 per month for inividual membership $15 per hour indoor and $10 per hour outdoor
hours: Monday - Friday: 8AM-6PM Saturday & Sunday: 9AM-2PM 24/7 access for members! MAGNOLIA PICKLEBALL HOURS Monday - Friday: 8AM-6PM Saturday & Sunday: 9AM-5PM 24/7 access for members! CONTACT US 547 Church Rd Suite G. Gluckstadt, MS 39110 info@magnoliapickleballms.com (601)-407-6057 Designed by Pittman Marketing

name: Reunion Country Club
description: Reunion Country Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Madison, MS. The court surface is hard. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Ridgeland Tennis Center
description: Ridgeland Tennis Center is a place to play pickleball in Ridgeland, MS. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms, a pro shop or equipment store, water, and lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: The Club at The Township
description: The Club at The Township is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Ridgeland, MS. There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Reservations are available. Amenities include restrooms, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, and water.

name: Wolcott Park
description: Come play pickleball at Wolcott Park in Ridgeland, MS! There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include a pro shop or equipment store, lights, wheelchair accessibility, restrooms, and food. The courts can be reserved. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — Court Fees are $4 for  1 hour and a half
